description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §16.2 specifies `admin.flush` to force fsync for a namespace (and optionally trigger a checkpoint). There is no admin flush handler or IPC/CLI surface; `src/daemon/admin.rs` only has status/metrics/doctor/scrub/reload/maintenance/rotate. This leaves no explicit manual durability/flush hook.

**Design**
- Add admin IPC op + CLI subcommand `bd admin flush [--namespace] [--checkpoint-now]` (namespace default core).
- Implement daemon handler to call WAL fsync for the namespace and optionally enqueue checkpoint for affected groups.
- Surface in `src/api` + renderers; include timing/durability info in response.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] IPC + CLI `admin flush` exists and returns success payload.
- [ ] WAL fsync performed for requested namespace.
- [ ] `--checkpoint-now` schedules a checkpoint for groups including that namespace.
- [ ] Tests cover handler + response shape.

**Files:** src/daemon/admin.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/render.rs, src/api
title: admin.flush missing (fsync + optional checkpoint trigger)

description:
**Problem**
HLC relies on wall clock time. If a machine clock jumps backward or is far ahead, its stamps can dominate LWW merges and effectively overwrite other actors' updates. This can look like data loss.

**Design**
Persist the last-seen WriteStamp (per-repo) and clamp local wall_ms to >= last_seen.wall_ms. If local time moves backwards, advance logical counter instead of wall time. Add skew detection (warn if now << last_seen or now >> last_seen by threshold). Consider storing max observed stamp in meta or WAL to survive restarts.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Stamps remain monotonic across restarts and backward clock jumps.
- [ ] Skew detection is surfaced (status/log).
- [ ] Tests simulate backward/forward jumps and verify monotonicity + merge ordering.

**Files:** src/daemon/clock.rs, src/core/time.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
title: Clock skew can break LWW ordering
Add require_live helper to eliminate bead lookup boilerplate

description:
**Problem**
Current Gas City writes and reads orchestration state through bead metadata. It calls `bd create --metadata`, `bd update --set-metadata`, `bd list --metadata-field`, uses graph-plan metadata/metadata_refs, and parses response metadata maps.

This is not a generic metadata feature request. The latest source shows durable workflow/control, convergence, session, graph-apply, idempotency, routing, convoy, and API projection state living behind those keys. Floor 1 needs an implementation-ready state-machine projection inventory: which current keys exist, which state machine owns them, how they project onto typed Rust state, and which adapter behavior must remain while Gas City is still metadata-shaped.

**Design**
Build a source-grounded projection inventory from the latest local Gas City checkout.

For each metadata key or prefix, record:

- current writer(s): `--metadata`, `--set-metadata`, `SetMetadata`, `SetMetadataBatch`, graph plan metadata/metadata_refs, close metadata, lifecycle transition patch, or API update;
- current reader(s): `bead.Metadata[...]`, `ListByMetadata`, dispatch runtime, convergence reconcile, session projection, API serialization, ready/filtering, etc.;
- owning state machine or substrate: workflow/control, convergence, session, convoy, graph apply, dep edge, namespace, runtime-only, derived, or explicit reject;
- projected Rust home: typed variant field, typed edge payload, namespace property, label, derived value, runtime-only state, or explicit reject;
- compatibility behavior: whether Gas City-shaped JSON must still include the key and how typed fields render back to the metadata map;
- merge semantics needed by the typed field: LWW cohesive struct, monotonic max, append log, claim/lease, OR-set, resettable counter, or none.

This bead should produce the implementation-ready map that workflow/control, convergence, session, dep, graph, and CLI bridge beads use. It is before code extraction of reusable CRDT helpers, but it should not describe those helpers as speculative when the source already proves a need.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Inventory is refreshed against `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity` at the current commit
- [ ] All current `--metadata`, `--set-metadata`, `--metadata-field`, `SetMetadata`, `SetMetadataBatch`, graph-plan metadata/metadata_refs, and lifecycle transition patch call sites are accounted for
- [ ] Each key/prefix has exactly one owning state machine/substrate and projected Rust home or an explicit rejection
- [ ] The projection distinguishes canonical typed state from Gas City compatibility JSON
- [ ] Unknown metadata-key policy is explicit
- [ ] `gc.control_epoch`, `gc.attempt_log`, convergence state, and session claim/lifecycle fields are classified as source-proven state, not vague primitive candidates
- [ ] Follow-up implementation beads are filed or existing Floor 1 beads are updated for any newly discovered blockers
- [ ] Verification includes source grep commands used to prove coverage

**References**
- `docs/go-parity/FLOOR1_REPLAN.md`
- `docs/go-parity/primitives/metadata-inventory.md`
- `docs/go-parity/primitives/metadata-remapping.md`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity/internal/beads/bdstore.go`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity/internal/dispatch/`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity/internal/convergence/`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity/internal/session/`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity/internal/molecule/`

notes.author: darin@darinsmcstudio2.lan
notes.content: 2026-05-01 partial audit from .2.1/.2.2: issue_type and dependency-kind vocabulary are now source-checked, but the exhaustive Gas City projection map is still open here. gc.kind remains a metadata discriminator for workflow/control roles such as workflow, scope, scope-check, workflow-finalize, retry, retry-run, retry-eval, ralph, run, check, fanout, spec, and cleanup; do not collapse those into BeadType without this inventory.

title: Floor 1: Gas City state-machine projection inventory
